overlay_components.role: cross-asset return std vs rolling-60 median

lineage.selection_rule: Identical to v_alpha_v1_lsk3 in score / signs / required_feature_columns / portfolio construction. Differs only in profile_constraints.position_multiplier_overlay_id: when set to "alpha_ontology_regime_gating_v1", the universe-wide regime multiplier in [0.30, 1.00] is multiplied into raw_target_weights at the portfolio sizing layer (execution_backtest._cross_sectional_period). Score function and gates are unchanged.

thesis_profile.market_mechanism: W3.5 regime-gated lsk3: same xs_alpha_ontology_v1_score (v91 9 + F33 + F12) and same long-short top-3 perp construction as v_alpha_v1_lsk3, with a position-size multiplier overlay applied at the portfolio sizing layer. The multiplier in [0.30, 1.00] is built from the three universe-wide regime gauges F49 + F26 + F44 (W3.1 / W3.2 / W3.3). Hypothesis: by reducing exposure during shock-cluster windows and low-dispersion periods (when idiosyncratic alpha is least extractable), the gated variant should improve regime_holdout worst-regime sharpe and walk_forward loss_window_fraction relative to the un-gated v_alpha_v1_lsk3. The multiplier average is ~0.71 over 2023-04 to 2026-04, so the gated variant runs at ~71% average exposure; absolute returns are expected to be lower but risk-adjusted metrics (worst regime, drawdown depth) should improve.
thesis_profile.directional_claim: Same long-short top-3 perp signed-z-score construction as v_alpha_v1_lsk3. Position size is then multiplied by the W3.5 regime gating multiplier in [0.30, 1.00]. Falsified if walk_forward median sharpe drops more than 0.5 below v_alpha_v1_lsk3 (+1.748) without a matching improvement in regime_holdout worst-regime sharpe (current -1.608 vs strict cap -2.00) OR if execution_stress max_trade_participation_rate stays below 0.005 cap (already cleanly under at lsk3 baseline) but the strategy fails strict_validation on any other section due to overlay throttling.
thesis_profile.factor_formula: raw = -0.20*z(iv_smooth_60) -0.10*z(rv_5) +0.18*z(dh_60) +0.15*z(dh_5) -0.07*z(tt_smooth_5) -0.10*z(liquidity_stress_qv_iv) -0.06*z(momentum_decay_5_20) +0.05*z(taker_imb_dispersion) -0.05*z(quality_funding_oi) +0.10*z(downside_upside_vol_ratio_30) +0.07*z(funding_basis_residual_implied_repo_30); final_score = tanh((percentile_rank(raw)-0.5)*1.80); target_weight_per_name = (long_leverage / top_K - short_leverage / bottom_K) * regime_gating_multiplier(t)

experimental_reason: W3.5 regime gating overlay v1 produces material walk-forward improvements (median +0.46 sharpe, loss_frac -6.3pp, stdev -1.29) and regime sign-consistency gain (positive_regime_fraction 33.3% -> 66.7%, 1/3 -> 2/3 regimes positive). However, worst-regime median OOS sharpe regresses from -1.608 to -2.243, dropping below the v10 contract floor of -2.0 and triggering strict_validation FAIL on regime_holdout. Diagnosis: the v1 overlay components (F49 shock fraction, F26 3-day cluster count, F44 return dispersion) are SHOCK-based and do not fire during slow-grinding regime windows (e.g. rotation_high_vol_2025q4). The strategy maintains full exposure during sustained-vol regimes and accumulates the loss without throttling. v2 of the overlay should add F55 (BTC vol regime quantile) and/or a trailing-strategy-drawdown gauge to capture sustained-vol patterns that shock-based metrics miss. lsk3_g remains preserved as audit evidence; v_alpha_v1_lsk3 stays the active strict-passing candidate.
